normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: No additional preconditions beyond having an array and a target.
The input slice/array is sorted in nondecreasing order.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (i : Nat),
  (h : i < arr.size) →
  (get? arr i = some target) →
  (binarySearch arr target = some i) ∨ (∃ j : Nat, j < arr.size ∧ get? arr j = some target ∧ binarySearch arr target = some j)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If there exists an index i in bounds with arr[i] = target, the algorithm returns Some at some index where arr[index] = target.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  (∀ i : Nat, i < arr.size → get? arr i ≠ some target) →
  binarySearch arr target = none
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the target does not occur anywhere in the array, the algorithm returns None.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (res : Option Nat),
  (binarySearch arr target = res) →
  (res = some (by
    intro i; exact i) → True)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Placeholder to ensure the result is an Option Nat (index) when Some is returned.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (i : Nat),
  binarySearch arr target = some i →
  i < arr.size ∧ get? arr i = some target
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns Some(i), then i is in bounds and arr[i] = target.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  binarySearch arr target = none →
  ∀ i : Nat, i < arr.size → get? arr i ≠ some target
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns None, then the target is absent from all in-bounds indices.
